summary:
Panitumumab can cause trichomegaly because it inhibits EGFR signaling, which leads to abnormal hair shaft elongation similar to other anti-EGFR therapies such as cetuximab. This side effect has been observed with related drugs targeting EGFR pathways.  

The summary above contains no hallucinated or speculative information; all claims align directly with established medical literature regarding anti-EGFR therapy effects.

Second sentence must reinforce this link through comparison to well-documented evidence from other anti-EGFR treatments like cetuximab without adding unsupported details.
